topic: Someone finds an unsigned birthday card in their mailbox
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail slot clattered as I pulled the stack of envelopes from the box. Amidst the glossy advertisements and utility bills, something caught my eye. It was a card, thick cardstock, completely blank save for a single, elegant script in the corner that looked like a hastily drawn flourish. It wasn't addressed to anyone, and there was no stamp. My fingers, usually steady, started to tremble slightly as I picked it up. I turned it over, searching for a hidden message, but there was nothing. A strange prickle ran down my arms. I glanced around the quiet living room, half expecting to see someone watching me. I placed the card down on the coffee table, the paper feeling strangely cold beneath my fingertips, and immediately felt a compelling urge to discard it, yet my hand remained hovering over it, rooted to the spot.

[story 2]

I was sorting through the morning mail, a routine task, when the card appeared. It was folded precisely, a cream-colored piece of paper bearing no sender's name or any celebratory greeting. My breath hitched. I stared at it, my jaw tightening. It felt deliberately placed, an intrusion into the mundane rhythm of my day. I picked it up, my knuckles whitening as I examined the edges. There was an unsettling perfection to the handwriting on the front—too graceful, too deliberate. I tucked it into my pocket, the fabric of my jeans suddenly feeling scratchy against my skin. I kept glancing toward the front door, listening intently for any sound that wasn't the usual city hum, a knot forming tight in my stomach that refused to loosen.

[story 3]

It was tucked deep within the pile of junk mail, almost lost beneath a flyer for a local bake sale. When I finally pulled it out, the card seemed to absorb the light. It was an unsigned birthday card, pristine white, with a single, small, almost invisible smudge near the bottom edge. I held it between my thumb and forefinger, my gaze darting around the hallway. Every shadow seemed deeper, every muffled sound amplified. Why was it here? Who had this? A cold wash spread through my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I quickly shoved the card into my wallet, the movement jerky and hurried, desperate to get rid of the object before I could process the strange weight of its presence.
